But dividing minutes by 60,000 would result in a fraction of a second unless minutes is a multiple of 60,000, which it's not because minutes are calculated as `offset / DateTimeConstants.MILLIS_PER_MINUTE`. So that would lead to incorrect calculations.

This change could be causing the time zone formatting to miscalculate the offset, leading to wrong values being used in other parts of the code. That explains why multiple tests related to date-time zones are failing now—they depend on correct offset calculations.
